posts.title: Premenstrual Syndrome (PMS): Red flag is coming!
posts.excerpt: Maybe you are having some signs before your period starts to happen. These signs are called Premenstrual Syndrome or PMS. It is a combined emotional and physical symptoms that most women get before their menstrual period starts and right after ovulation. Generally, it comes with physical pain and feeling enigma. Having PMS with unbearable pain like headaches and moodiness can sometimes lead you to miss work or school. These are mostly to be experienced by young adolescents up to ‘30s of women. However, PMS symptoms stop after menopause.
posts.html:
n

Maybe you are having some signs before your period starts to happen. These signs are called Premenstrual Syndrome or PMS. It is a combined emotional and physical symptoms that most women get before their menstrual period starts and right after ovulation. Generally, it comes with physical pain and feeling enigma.

Having PMS with unbearable pain like headaches and moodiness can sometimes lead you to miss work or school. These are mostly to be experienced by young adolescents up to ‘30s of women. However, PMS symptoms stop after menopause.

nnnn

Signs and Symptoms of PMS

nnnn

For physical symptoms, these are the following:

nnnn
  • Headache and backache
  • Joint and muscle pain
  • Tenderness of breasts
  • Gain weight
  • Clumsiness
  • Fatigue
  • Acne
nnnn

While emotional or behavioral symptoms can include:

nnnn
  • Mood swings & irritability (most common or severe symptoms of PMS)
  • Anxiety and/or depression
  • Less social or inactive
  • Lack of concentration
  • Sleep problems
  • Food cravings
  • Feeling tired
nnnn

Causes of PMS

nnnn

There are no specific causes of PMS, but changes in hormone levels during the menstrual cycle may play a role. And these may also include:

nnnn
  • Smoking
  • Lack of sleep
  • Depressed
  • No physical activities or exercise
  • Drinking too much alcohol and coffee
  • Eating too much salty or sugary food

nnnn

Tips to Prevent PMS Symptoms

nnnn

To prevent having PMS you can practice the following:

nnnn
  • Eat healthy food
  • Avoid too much salty food, caffeine and alcohol
  • Proper exercise at least 30 minutes a day
  • Get plenty of sleep
  • Get enough rest
nnnn

nnThe prevention tips mentioned above will be to great help for those women who suffer from symptoms. PMS does not have a test for diagnosis, but for those who cannot endure the pain or with severe symptoms must see a doctor.nn

nnnn

References:

nnnn

Dela Cruz, Y., 2012 Salamat Dok: PMS: It's that time of the month! Retrieved from https://news.abs-cbn.com/current-affairs-programs/02/27/12/salamat-dok-pms-it%E2%80%99stime-month

nnnn

Premenstrual Syndrome (PMS), 2015 Retrieved from https://www.acog.org/Patients/FAQs/Premenstrual-Syndrome-PMS?IsMobileSet=false#make

nnnn

Premenstrual syndrome (PMS), 2018 Retrieved from https://www.womenshealth.gov/menstrualcycle/premenstrual-syndrome

nnnn

Premenstrual syndrome (PMS), 2018 Retrieved from https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseasesconditions/premenstrual-syndrome/symptoms-causes/syc-20376780

nnnn

nnStoppler, M., MD, Retrieved fromnhttps://www.medicinenet.com/premenstrual_syndrome/article.htm#how_common_is_pms_premnenstrual_syndromenn

n

posts.title: What can you get when it’s cold? – Sore Throat
posts.excerpt: The rainy season has something to do with our health condition. As the virus gets active during rainy or cold weather, one needs to be more cautious. One of the common ailments is a sore throat. Here are some insights about having a sore throat and some of its remedies.
posts.html:
n

The rainy season has something to do with our health condition. As the virus gets active during rainy or cold weather, one needs to be more cautious. One of the common ailments is a sore throat. Here are some insights about having a sore throat and some of its remedies.

nnnn

Sore throat, or also called Pharyngitis, is a painful, scratchy or irritated throat. It is caused by swelling in the back of the throat (pharynx) between tonsils and the voice box (larynx). Children and teens are most likely to develop a sore throat.

Causes of Sore Throat
Sore throats are commonly caused by viruses, bacteria or fungal. Can include:

nnnn
  • Common cold
  • Flu (influenza)
  • Allergies (molds, dust or even pet dander)
  • Irritants (smoking, eating cold & spicy food, drinking alcohol)
  • Strep throats
  • Tonsillitis
nnnn

Symptoms of Sore Throat
Symptoms can vary depending on the cause.

nnnn
  • Pain and scratchy sensation of throat
  • Swollen, redness of tonsils
  • Difficulty or pain in swallowing
  • White spots or pus in tonsils (usually caused by strep throat)
  • Painful neck or jaw
nnnn

In some cases, the following signs and symptoms may occur:

nnnn
  • Fever
  • Headache
  • Cough
  • Sneezing
  • Hoarse voice
  • Joint or muscle pain
  • Vomiting
  • Loss of appetite
nnnn

Home Remedies for Sore Throat

nnnn

It is recommended to have full rest to help the immune system fight the infection. Here are some ways to ease or relieve the pain.

nnnn
  • Drink warm liquids frequently to keep throat from getting dry (preferably water, tea with honey or soup broth).
  • Gargle with warm salt water. (1 tsp. salt, 1 cup water & stir)
  • Moisture the air with cool-mist humidifier.
  • Suck on a hard candy or an over-the-counter lozenge
nnnn

Note: Taking antibiotics and anti-allergy must have a prescription. In most cases, sore throat gets better within a few days on their own. However, some patients must see a doctor if it gets worst.

nnnn

Preventive tips for Sore Throat
Sore throat can be prevented by:

nnnn
  • Washing hands regularly
  • Covering when sneezing or coughing
  • Using alcohol-based hand sanitizers frequently
  • Cleaning phones, telephone, TV remotes and computer keyboards often
  • Avoiding sharing of food utensils
nnnn

Sore throats, strep throats, and tonsillitis are often used interchangeably by many, yet they differ from one another. Tonsillitis refers to inflamed tonsils, while strep throat is an infection caused by a specific type of bacteria called Streptococcus. And, sore throat is the inflammation of the pharynx which in between tonsils and larynx.

nnnn

References:

nnnn

Dhakal. S., 2015 Sore throat: Viral, bacterial infection most common during seasonal changes. Retrieved from https://thehimalayantimes.com/lifestyle/health/sore-throat-viral-bacterialinfection-most-common-during-seasonal-changes/

Sore Throat, 2017 Retrieved from https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/sorethroat/symptoms-causes/syc-20351635 Sore Throat, 2019 familydoctor.org. Retrieved from https://familydoctor.org/condition/sorethroat/?adfree=true

Vorvick, L., MD, et. al., 2017 Pharyngitis - sore throat. Retrieved from https://medlineplus.gov/ency/article/000655.htm

n

posts.title: Small Step to Weight Loss
posts.excerpt: So, you’ve made your choice to trade off your sedentary days to a healthier, more active, youth-living lifestyle. Although this could be such an exciting change for you, it could be a little bit overwhelming especially when you try to apply big things you haven’t tried with yourself before. So here, we’ve listed down a few things which you can start with before you tread heavily on your body goal.
posts.html:
n

So, you’ve made your choice to trade off your sedentary days to a healthier, more active, youth-living lifestyle. Although this could be such an exciting change for you, it could be a little bit overwhelming especially when you try to apply big things you haven’t tried with yourself before. So here, we’ve listed down a few things which you can start with before you tread heavily on your body goal.

nnnn
  • NEVER skip Breakfast
nnnn

A lot of people who decides to lose weight tends to skip their morning meals, too, thinking that missing breakfast could cut off calories from their daily intake. But the real thing is, it doesn’t really cut off anything. People who don’t eat breakfasts tend to overeat later in that day, causing extra more unexpected calorie intake. So, lesson: Eat your breakfast.

nnnn
  • Walk, walk, walk
nnnn

Walking is anlow-stress exercise and an excellent fat burner. You may start offering to picknup a colleague’s paper from the photocopier and gain a friend. Stretch yournlegs! Your start can be as simple as that. Choose ways that involve physicalneffort, like opting to use the stairs rather than the elevator.

nnnn

Slowly butnsurely, you will reach your body goal.

nnnn
  • Practice mindful eating
nnnn

Thosen“pika-pika” or finger foods you hide below your desk can be a huge culprit tonyour weight loss plan. Practice mindful eating by refraining from giving in tonthese kinds of snacks like flavored cornicks and chips. Instead, pack upnhealthy, low-calorie Don’t get confused with hunger and boredom.

nnnn
  • Consume more water
nnnn

People alsonoften confuse thirst with hunger. Maybe it’s just water that you need. Othernthan that, drinking water can also boost your metabolism and help you burn offna few more calories. You may also try consuming a glass of water before yournmeals, this may help you eat less of food.

nnnn
  • Brush your teeth after every meal
nnnn

That clean andnminty freshness lets your brain know that mealtime is over.

nnnn
  • Think about your motivation
nnnn

Keep remindingnyourself why you want to lose weight, this can help you keep going and pushingnforward. Write down your short term and long term goals to make it appear muchneasier to attain. Set your goal high and write down every step and achievement.n

n

posts.title: Protect Your Eyes from the Summer Heat: Avoid Sore Eyes
posts.excerpt: Summer lasts for two to three months in the country and it is also the season for many eye illnesses. While the sweltering summer heat may scald and irritate our skin, our eyes are no exception. As conjunctivitis, or sore eyes, commonly appears during summertime, the Department of Health (DOH) has mentioned that it may still occur during the rainy season.
posts.html:
n

Summer lasts for two to three months in the country and it is also the season for many eye illnesses. While the sweltering summer heat may scald and irritate our skin, our eyes are no exception. As conjunctivitis, or sore eyes, commonly appears during summertime, the Department of Health (DOH) has mentioned that it may still occur during the rainy season.

nnnn

The basic practice to avoidngetting sore eyes is to never rub or touch your eyes with unclean hands. Poornhygiene may also introduce us to bacteria that may potentially causeninfections, it also speeds up the spread of the virus. Do not share eyencosmetics, as well as the objects you put near your eyes like eyeglasses andnsunglasses, eye makeups and face towels. Use goggles when swimming to prevent exposurento germs that might be in the water.

nnnn

One thing also to remember is avoidingnpeople who have conjunctivitis since the virus can spread to the objects thatnperson has touched.

nnnn

Signs and Symptoms

nnnn
  • Redness in the white of the eye or inner eyelid
  • Swollennconjunctiva
  • Increasednamount of tears
  • Thicknyellow, green or white eye discharge
  • Crustingndischarges over the eyelashes
  • Itchy eyes
  • Burning eyes
  • More sensitive to light
  • Swollen lymph nodes
nnnn

Quick Remedies

nnnn
  • Avoid wearing contact lenses. Get a new pair ofncontact lenses once you get better as your old contacts are likely infected andncould get you sick if you wear them again.
  • Wash your hands more often, especially beforenand after you touch your eyes
  • Change pillowcases and bed linens every day.
  • To avoid the virus from spreading over, wash thentowels and beddings with hot water and detergent.
  • Never share your eye cosmetics.
  • Use warm compress 3 to 4 times a day. This wouldnease the pain and helps break up some of the formed crust on your eyelashes.
  • Do not overuse and share your eye drops withnanybody.
nnnn

If you are feeling the following, come and see a doctor right away

nnnn
  • When the pain and itchiness increases
  • Intense redness of the eyes
  • Sensitivity to light or blurred vision
  • Symptoms get worse or don’t improve
  • Pre-existing eye conditions that may put you atnrisk for complications and severe infection
nnnn
nnnn
n
n
n

Sources:

nhttps://www.webmd.com/eye-health/features/5-ways-to-protect-your-eyes-in-summer#1
nhttps://www.allaboutvision.com/conditions/burning-eyes.htm
nhttps://www.indiatimes.com/health/healthyliving/common-eye-problems-to-prevent-this-summer-241278.html
nhttp://www.rmc.doh.gov.ph/patientscorner/health-corner/137-sore-eyes
nhttps://ph.theasianparent.com/prevent-kids-having-sore-eyes-summer
nhttps://www.goodeyes.com/blog/10-ways-to-protect-your-eyes-this-summer/
nhttps://www.philstar.com/headlines/2004/08/19/261843/doh-warning-watch-out-sore-eyes-rainy-season
nhttps://www.webmd.com/eye-health/eye-health-conjunctivitis#1
nhttps://www.cdc.gov/conjunctivitis/about/prevention.html
nhttps://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/pink-eye/symptoms-causes/syc-20376355n

posts.title: How to Prevent and Treat Heat Rash
posts.excerpt: One of the common cases of summernillnesses peaking in the country is miliarianrubra, which is commonly known as heatnrash. It is often associated with babies and children but still can occurnto anyone, especially in the stifling summer heat. People who are overweight,nand those who sweat so easily, are also more likely to get heat rash.
posts.html:
n

One of the common cases of summernillnesses peaking in the country is miliarianrubra, which is commonly known as heatnrash. It is often associated with babies and children but still can occurnto anyone, especially in the stifling summer heat. People who are overweight,nand those who sweat so easily, are also more likely to get heat rash.

nnnn
nnnn

When sweat glands are blocked, the sweat produced cannot get to the surface of the skin to evaporate, which causes inflammation that appears as reddened skin with tiny blisters. These come with an itchy, prickly feeling, and may also swell mildly. Heat rash may appear on the back, under the arms, near the groin, and in the neck.

nnnn

Preventing heat rash

nnnn
  • Keep your skin cool by wearing loose cotton clothing,nand avoid overdressing.
  • Take cool baths or showers.
  • Use lightweight bedding for a cooler, morencomfortable sleep. Keep your sleeping area cool and well-ventilated.
  • Drink plenty of fluid to avoid dehydration.
nnnn

Heat rash home treatments

nnnn
  • Wash the reddened area with cool water and mild soap. Allow your skin to air dry, and avoid rubbing with a towel as it may irritate it more. Allow the skin to cool off by remaining in a well-ventilated, cool area.
  • Use an aloe vera gel on your heat rash to relieve discomfort, this can help soothe swelling and pain.
  • Oatmeal is also an effective remedy which can be readily available in your home. It helps relieve itching and inflammation of heat rash and a number of other skin conditions.
nnnn

Put 1 or 2 cups of oatmeal in a lukewarm bath and soak the affected area for 20 minutes. Afterward, allow your skin to air dry.

nnnn
  • You may also use a calamine lotion to help soothe the itchiness.
nnnn

Should the symptoms become severe, instantly seek advice from your doctor.

nnnn
nnnn


nnnnn

Sources:

nhttps://www.nhs.uk/conditions/heat-rash-prickly-heat/
nhttps://www.healthline.com/health/heat-rash-home-remedies#Home-remedies-for-heat-rash-
nhttps://medshadow.org/heat-rash/
nhttps://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/181512.php
n

n

posts.title: UTI: Here’s what you can do to prevent it
posts.excerpt: Having the frequent need to pee? Or that burning sensation while urinating? If you are feeling both of these symptoms, perhaps you may have a urinary tract infection (UTI). According to Mayoclinic, UTI is an infection in any part of your urinary system—your kidneys, ureters, bladder, and urethra.
posts.html:
Having the frequent need to pee? Or that burning sensation while urinating? If you are feeling both of these symptoms, perhaps you may have a urinary tract infection (UTI). According to Mayoclinic, UTI is an infection in any part of your urinary system—your kidneys, ureters, bladder, and urethra.

n

UTI is seen mostly in women, while the reason behind is not completely understood, experts do know that the way a woman’s body is put together creates a perfect setup for bacteria to enter the urinary tract.

n

Thankfully, UTIs are very easily treated by antibiotics, but what do you do if you are someone who wants to be proactive?

n

Here are some basic ways that can help you put UTI at bay.

nnn
  • Drink plenty of water
nnnn

Drinking water helps dilute your urine and ensures that you'll urinate more frequently — allowing bacteria to be flushed from your urinary tract before an infection can begin.

nnnn
  • Urinate frequently
nnnn

Don’t hold it in. Flush out the bacteria out of your urinary tract.

nnnn
  • Wipe from front to back
nnnn

This helps prevent bacteria in the anal region from spreading to the vagina and urethra.

nnnn
  • Try avoid or limit consumption of food that irritate the bladder
nnnn

This includes sugar, processed and pre-packaged ready-made items, caffeine, alcohol, dairy products, red meat and other animal protein, fish, lots of corn, wheat, and potatoes.

nnnn
  • Urinate promptly before and after intercourse
nnnn

This may help flush out bacteria that may have been introduced during intercourse. Wash the genital area with warm water before having sex.

nnnn
  • Avoid tight-fitting clothing
nnnn

Wear cotton garments for maximum breathability.

nnnn
  • Observe your pee
nnnn

Your urine can tell so much about what’s going on inside your bladder—its clarity and color variety. So, pay close attention to your pee.

nnnn


Once you are feeling something different, don’t hesitate to tell. Seek your doctor’s care instantly.

nnnn



Source:

https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/urinary-tract-infection/symptoms-causes/syc-20353447
https://www.everydayhealth.com/urinary-tract-infections/the-link-between-utis-and-sex.aspx
https://www.healthline.com/health/chronic-urinary-tract-infection#prevention
https://www.cystex.com/about-urinary-tract-infections/how-do-i-prevent-utis/
https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/9135-urinary-tract-infections/prevention
https://uqora.com/pages/uti-prevention

nnnn

n

posts.title: Tips for a Healthy Heart
posts.excerpt: Live your life with a better heart health
posts.html:
n

Live your life with a better heart health

nnnn
nnn

Time does not exempt our bodies, we all age. As we get older, our vital organs begin to function less well. Our cells experience a gradual decline and become less able to divide and multiply. And our heart is not an exception.

Here’s the truth, time does not shift backward; we will never grow back and get younger. What we can do is to continually look after our bodies and modify our lifestyles.

There are many steps that you can do to maintain a good and healthy heart. Here are some of them that you can easily start with.

nnn

Give up the butts

nnnn
nnnn

Smoking has a huge impact on your heart health. It increases the stiffness of the blood vessels making it harder for them to expand and contract as needed and more likely to split. So if you’re a smoker, make an effort to give it up now.

nnnn

Keep moving

nnnn

Getting and staying active can reduce your risk of developing heart diseases. It can also be a great mood booster and stress buster.

nnnn

Eat more fiber

nnnn

Eat plenty of fiber to help lower your risk of heart disease – aim for at least 30g a day. 

nnnn

Cut down on salt and saturated fat

nnnn

Taking too much salt, as well as saturated fat, over time can increase your risk of high blood pressure, which is a major risk factor for heart disease. For a healthy heart, it is best to reduce your salt intake.

nnnn

Drink less alcohol

nnnn

Regular drinking more than the recommended alcohol intake can result to a greater risk of serious problems with your health, especially your heart.

nnnn

Eat fish

nnnn

Eat fish at least twice a week, including a portion of oily fish. Fish such as pilchards, sardines and salmon are a source of omega-3 fats, which may help protect against heart disease.

nnnn



Source: 

https://www.heartfoundation.org.au/your-heart/know-your-risks/smoking-and-your-heart
https://www.heartfoundation.org.au/healthy-eating/food-and-nutrition/salt
https://www.healthline.com/health/healthy-heart-tips#find-your-happy-place
https://www.nhs.uk/live-well/healthy-body/

n

posts.title: Words to Help Someone Who is Depressed
posts.excerpt: It isn’t always that easy to find words of comfort for a friend or a family who is going through his rough time. The fear of saying the wrong words, or speaking at the wrong time, makes you feel unsure and hesitant to be expressive of what you truly meant to say. But don’t let this dissuade you—your love and care doesn’t have to be profoundly written and well-rehearsed.
posts.html:
n

It isn’t always that easy to find words of comfort for a friend or a family who is going through his rough time. The fear of saying the wrong words, or speaking at the wrong time, makes you feel unsure and hesitant to be expressive of what you truly meant to say. But don’t let this dissuade you—your love and care doesn’t have to be profoundly written and well-rehearsed.

nnnn

When you are at loss of words, you may try to express these statements that someone who is depressed might find very helpful to hear.

nnnn

“I Care”
These two simple words are strong enough to communicate your love for them. Surprise them with a hug or a pat on the back can send a message across that they matter.

nnnn

“I’m Here for You”
A common feeling of those who have depression is that they have been left and they are alone in this world. Let them know they aren’t, make them feel that they can count on you.

nnnn

“Your Life Makes a Difference”
Remind him of his value and worth, and the memories you both have shared together. This is a great way, even by little by little, to encourage them that life could get better.

nnnn

“It’s OK to Feel This Way”
One thing that this can teach you, too, is that everyone has his own road to tread, his own time to manage.
So never judge. Healing doesn’t come around in a single day. Let them take their time, reassure them that you will always be there by their side despite the circumstances.

nnnn

“You Aren’t Weak”
Those who are coping with depression tend to feel weak or that there is something wrong with them. Depression may be seen as an illness to others, but those who live with it feel that it's a character flaw.
Reassure your friend or your relative that depression really is an illness caused by a biochemical imbalance in the brain, and it does not mean that he is weak. In fact, it takes a great deal of strength to fight back, so he is probably much stronger than the average person.

nnnn

Depression is a serious matter. It takes work to get out of it, but it is so worth the effort. Feeling some joy when you’ve been down is really a breath of fresh air. So help them out. Be there when they need a helping hand, a shoulder to lean on to, or someone to listen to when they talk. And make sure to always remind them that they matter.

nnnn

nnnn



Sources:

https://www.verywellmind.com/what-to-say-when-someone-is-depressed-1067474
https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/blog/emotional-fitness/201803/10-things-people-depression-need-do-every-day

n

posts.title: Nutrients for the Ears: Improving Your Hearing Health
posts.excerpt: With a balanced diet and the right types of food, we can help our ears get the nourishment they need. Here are the top nutrients good for your hearing health:
posts.html:
n

With a balanced diet and the right types of food, we can help our ears get the nourishment they need. Here are the top nutrients good for your hearing health:

nnnn
  • POTASSIUM
nnnn

Potassium helps regulate fluid in our blood and tissues, as our inner ear needs potassium as part of its crucial process of converting the noise that we hear into impulses that our brain interprets as sound.

nnnn

As we get older,nour body parts age including our auditory system and the level of potassiumnnaturally drops which contributes to the causes of age-related hearing lossn(A-RHL).

nnnn

Potassium-richnfoods include bananas, potatoes, yogurt, raisins, avocados, mangoes, tomatoes,nand milk.

nnnn
  • FOLIC ACID
nnnn
nnnn

Folic acid plays a critical role in your body’s generation of new cell growth and helps increase circulation in the body. It contributes to the overall health of the hair cells in your inner ear.

nnnn

Foods rich in folic acid are liver, spinach, broccoli, and asparagus.




nnnn
  • OMEGA 3
nnnn
nnnn

Consumption of Omega 3 helps prevent some chronic diseases. It is also found to be useful in delaying development of A-RHL. Studies have shown that adults who consume fish twice a week had a much lower chance of facing A-RHL than non-fish eaters. Researchers claim that Omega 3 strengthen blood vessels in our ear’s sensory system. Omega 3 helps on the function of brain and ears more effectively.

nnnn

Great sources ofnOmega 3 are salmon, sardines, mackerel, walnuts, and chia seeds.

nnnn
  • ZINC
nnnn

Zinc is responsible for strengthening the immune system. It increases cell growth and heal wounds, and can help ward off germs that cause colds and ear infections.

nnnn

Foods rich innzinc include beef, pork and dark-meat chicken, peanuts and dark chocolates.

nnnn
  • MAGNESIUM
nnnn
nnnn

A study shows that individuals treated with magnesium in combination with Vitamins A, C, and E were protected to high levels of noise—and this deprives the development of A-RHL. It is believed that the magnesium acted as a protective barrier to hair cells in the inner ear when loud noises were emitted.

nnnn

Magnesium-richnfoods include spinach, potatoes, tomatoes, and broccoli.

nnnn






Sources:

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4975785/
http://fluxxaudio.com/food-for-your-ears/
https://www.healthyhearing.com/report/51181-Boost-your-hearing-with-these-power-foods
https://campaignforbetterhearing.ca/2016/03/five-foods-to-boost-your-hearing-health/
https://www.health24.com/Medical/Hearing-management/News/4-foods-that-can-improve-your-hearing-20180518
https://naroaudiology.com/blog/top-4-foods-for-healthy-ears-
https://www.miracle-ear.com/blog/vitamins-for-ear-health

n

posts.title: Healthy Changes to Start with
posts.excerpt: Lead a happier, healthier lifenand you got to start today. Here, we’ve provided you easy steps to make it allnhappen.
posts.html:
n

Lead a happier, healthier lifenand you got to start today. Here, we’ve provided you easy steps to make it allnhappen.

nnnn

Stay hydrated

nnnn

Staying hydrated is a good way tonavoid retaining waste. Water also helps transports nutrients providing energynacross the body. So, for a healthier life, drink more water!

nnnn

Avoid skipping meals

nnnn

Skipping meals tend to make you hungrier that you end up overeating later in the day.

nnnn

Get more good sleep

nnnn

With inadequate sleep, our bodyngoes into a state of stress, thus, puts us in a higher risk of heart attack,nstroke, and even depression. It would also be more difficult to take in newninformation. Sleep plays a critical role in vital functions such as immune function,nmetabolism, and memory and learning. Sleep is the time for our entire body tonrelax, encouraging production of protein molecules forming building blocks forncells, allowing to repair the damage made over the day. Don’t just take ansleep… take a good sleep.

nnnn

Encourage healthy, clean eating

nnnn
nnnn

Consume more fruit, vegetables,nwhole grains, nuts and seeds, and reduce consumption of processed and junknfoods. Stick to cleaner, greener diet and eliminate everything sugary, fatty,ngreasy and salty.

nnnn

Get involved in exercises

nnnn

Exercise plays a vital role innyour physical wellbeing. Go for a walk! Take the stairs and sneak extra stepsneach day. By getting active, you can reduce the risk of heart diseases andnstroke, and keep your weight under control.

nnnn

Reduce alcohol consumption

nnnn

A sip or few may not cause anynconcern to your health, but the cumulative effect of drinking alcohol can be.nIt increases the risk of for chronic liver inflammation and liver diseases. Andnover time, may also damage your frontal lobe which is the area of the brainnresponsible for emotional control, short-term memory and judgement.

nnnn

Quit smoking

nnnn

Cigarettes contains about 600 ingredients which when burns, generates more than 7,000 harmful and poisonous chemicals that some are linked to cancer. Smoking nearly harms every organ of the body; it multiplies the risk of developing cancer, heart diseases and other diseases. To have a healthy life, live with a healthy lifestyle.

nnnn

Sources:

https://www.verywellhealth.com/
https://www.healthline.com/
http://biggestloserresort.com/
https://www.webmd.com/
https://www.jillianmichaels.com/blog/health-and-fitness/
https://www.gaiam.com/
http://healthysleep.med.harvard.edu/healthy/

nnnn

nnnn

n

posts.title: Public reminded about cheaper, quality drugs
posts.excerpt: As the country reels from the inflated prices of food and services and other essential needs, local drug companies Innogen Pharmaceuticals, Inc., Getz Pharma (Phils.), Inc. and Zydus Healthcare Philippines, Inc. are reminding the public they have long made available the medications that provide effective yet very affordable relief to osteoarthritis, one of their major health problems.
posts.html:
n

As the country reels from the inflated prices of food and services and other essential needs, local drug companies Innogen Pharmaceuticals, Inc., Getz Pharma (Phils.), Inc. and Zydus Healthcare Philippines, Inc. are reminding the public they have long made available the medications that provide effective yet very affordable relief to osteoarthritis, one of their major health problems.

nnnn

nnThese medications are coxigen of Innogen Pharma, starcox of Getz Pharma and etoxi of Zydus Healthcare. nn

nnnn

These three are innovative, generic versions of etoricoxib, a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory medicine that counters the debilitating effects of osteoarthritis, the most common form of arthritis that afflicts millions of Filipinos, especially those aged 60 and above.

nnnn

The three companies issued the reminder as they expressed concern over the financial burden imposed by this ailment on the poor, especially on those living in areas severely damaged by Typhoon “Rosita” in end-October, and of Typhoon “Ompong” that also devastated various parts of the country just six weeks earlier.

nnnn

Osteoarthritis causes severe pain and prolonged swelling of joints in the hips, knees, hands, wrists, shoulders, neck and back.

nnnn

The condition can result in disability and is aggravated by excessive body weight, advanced age, injury to the joint, and the type of jobs that call for kneeling or lifting of heavy loads. In certain cases, the disease is caused by genetic or hereditary factors. 

nnnn

The ailment is said to affect more people than heart disease and hypertension combined.

nnnn

Innogen sells the drug in doses of 60mg, 90mg, and 120mg. 

nnnn

Priced at P35.85, P39.60 and P49.20 per tablet, respectively, the costs are at least 40­­ percent lower than the branded version manufactured by a foreign company. 

nnnn

Prices of coxigen, etoxi and starcox differ only very slightly and are actually negligible, but all three are within affordable range of even the poor victims of osteoarthritis.

nnnn

On the other hand, the foreign brand manufacturer reportedly sells its products at P59.75 for its 60mg dose tablet, P66 for the 90mg dose, and P82 for the 120mg dose.

nnnn

The three pharma firms said their version of etoricoxib answers the call for the availability of pharmaceuticals at prices that make them easily accessible to the public but whose quality and efficacy are comparable to, if not better than, imported foreign brands.

nnnn

They said their products conform to quality standards prescribed by health authorities and government regulatory bodies and are compliant with the provisions and requirements of the Cheaper Medicines Act (Rep. Act 9502).

nnnn

At the moment, the three drugs are available mostly in independent drug outlets due to patent rights claims of the foreign drug company that manufactures the branded, main competing product. Reportedly, this foreign drug firm has been harassing independent drug stores by accusing them of patent infringement and demanding that they cease and desist from carrying and selling the generic drugs.

nnnn

On the basis of alleged patent invalidity, Innogen has filed a petition for cancellation of the foreign drug firm’s patent with the Intellectual Property Office (IPO) where the issue is awaiting resolution.

nnnn

But even as it wished for positive results on its pending petition with the IPO, Innogen expressed the hope that for the sake of the poor victims of osteoarthritis, the major drug outlets will also carry the three local drugs and make these cheaper medications more readily available to the public.

nnnn

Source:http://manilastandard.net/news/national/281741/public-reminded-about-cheaper-quality-drugs.html?fbclid=IwAR14Tu9xpdcEw0F3MZ9LHV9VyoOWTQZFV4O3DY5Re6QGktY8YsB3pKfxXo8

n

posts.title: HOW TO: BIR Registration for Filipino Doctors
posts.excerpt: BIR registration can be as daunting and overwhelming at times, nonetheless, extremely necessary. Here’s the BIR registration guide laid out for doctors/physicians to get to know about tax filing and payment.
posts.html:
n

BIR registration can be as daunting and overwhelming at times, nonetheless, extremely necessary. Here’s the BIR registration guide laid out for doctors/physicians to get to know about tax filing and payment.

nnnn

This registration enables you to get to fully benefit from self-employment. Before we proceed with the registration process, make sure that you have all your documents prepared. Make a check list to secure you have them all with you!

nnnn

General Registration Requirements

nnnn
  • NSO Birth Certificate or any other government-issued valid ID that shows your name, address, and birth date (Original and Photocopy)
  • BIR Form No. 1901 (3 copies)
  • BIR Form No. 1906 (3 copies)
  • Final & clear sample of Principal Receipts and Invoices
  • BIR Form 0605 (4 copies)
  • Proof of Payment from BIR Form 0605 (original and photocopy)
  • Proof of Address
  • PRC license/ID
  • Payment of Professional Tax Receipt
  • Tax Identification Number (TIN)
nnnn

Additional Documentary Requirements (Subject to Which Taxpayer You Fall Under)

nnnn

BIR requires additional documentary requirements subject to which type of income earner you fall under as a physician/doctor.  

nnnn
Self-Employed PhysiciansMixed-Employed Physicians
BIR Form 19062 columnar books
DTI Certificate of Business Name (original or photocopy)Certificate of Employment
Contract of Lease
Mayor’s business Permit 
If applicable:
Marriage Contract (if married)Marriage Contract (if married)
Photocopy of birth certificate of dependents –for tax deduction (Criteria: 1) minimum of 4 children and 2) all children must be below 21 years old)Duly-filled  BIR Form No. 1905 (3 copies)- in case of transfer of TIN
Photocopy of birth certificate of dependents –for tax deduction (Criteria: 1) minimum of 4 children and 2) all children must be below 21 years old)
nnnn

Doctors / Physicians may be classified under the following taxpayer types:  

nnnn

Employed individuals are those who derive their income by performing activities or rendering services necessary for the business of their employer. Fundamentally, resident and government doctors are often classified as employed.  And for the most part, lucky them, residents and government doctors need not go through the BIR registration process, as their employer (government/hospital) takes charge of doing it on their behalf.

nnnn

Self-Employed Individuals, as defined by BIR, are:

nnnn

1) people who own a business and derive income from such business;

nnnn

2) people who make a living through their art, or; 

nnnn

3) professionals who earn by practicing their profession and are registered with the PRC. That being said, all physicians who started private practice are considered self-employed by BIR, for they fall under the professional category.  

nnnn

Mixed-Income Earners fall under any two of the following: Professionals, Single Proprietors, and Local Employees. They may be privately employed, but they may also have their own business or offer a professional service. 

nnnn

12-step BIR Registration Guide

nnnn

STEP #1: TIN Registration/TIN Update

nnnn

a. For doctors/physicians who have no prior experience working/not registered with the BIR

nnnn

You are required to obtain a Tax Identification Number (TIN). BIR offers an eRegistration system for TIN issuance. Visit the link for BIR eRegistration, fill out all pertinent taxpayer information, and confirm your email. Doing so will automatically register you as self-employed.

nnnn

NOTE: Your electronic TIN will be sent to your email.  Having multiple TIN is against the law.  So if you are unsure if you have an existing TIN, just call BIR’s hotline number (02) 981 8888.  You can also visit any BIR branch near you.

nnnn

b. For doctors/physicians who were employed, and want to transition from being employed to self-employed 

nnnn

You must update your TIN and apply for a TIN transfer. To do this, you must submit to your prevailing RDO /RDO of your previous employer 1) 3 copies of duly filled Form 1905 and 2) 3 copies of duly filled BIR Form 1901. Your employer was the one who enrolled you, or got the TIN on your behalf, so your records are on his/her RDO. Submit these forms together with your 3) Professional Tax Receipt, 4) DTI Certificate, 5) Mayor’s business Permit, 6) Contract of Lease, 7) NSO Birth Certificate, and 8) Marriage Contract (if married).

nnnn

The processing of TIN transfer typically takes 5 working days. And in times like these, follow-up questions are anticipated. So don’t forget to ask for the officer’s contact number before proceeding to your new RDO.

nnnn

NOTE: As has been noted, you can obtain a copy of the BIR forms at any RDO, but you can also download them online. For downloadable copies, refer to the links above. To reiterate, do not forget to set the forms in legal size.

nnnn

STEP #2: Submission of Requirements

nnnn

Go to your designated RDO for the verification of taxpayer information and the submission of the BIR forms and the documentary requirements specified above.

nnnn

STEP #3: Stamping of BIR Form 0605

nnnn

After submitting, the officer-in-charge will ask you to pay for the annual registration fee. But before that, you must get your copies of duly-filled BIR Form 0605 stamped by a BIR attendant.

nnnn

STEP #4: Payment of Registration fee

nnnn

After this, present your stamped copies of BIR Form 0605 (4 copies) to a BIR-authorized agent bank. Professionals and mixed-income earners are required to pay a P500 registration fee, so prepare to pay for this fee to the bank. Refer to this for BIR’s list of authorized agent banks. (hyperlink to: https://www.bir.gov.ph/index.php/list-of-authorized-agent-banks.html)

nnnn

NOTE: Do not misplace your official receipt as it would serve as your proof of payment. Obtain 2 photocopies of the receipt as well.  Moreover, you need to secure 2 photocopies of the stamped BIR form 0605 given back to you by the bank. You will need these two later.

nnnn

STEP 5:  Presenting your Proof of Payment

nnnn

Go back to the RDO to present your proof of payment. Submit your photocopies of the official receipt and the BIR Form 0605 given back to you by the bank.

nnnn

STEP #6: Getting your ATP (Authority to Print) Receipts/Invoices

nnnn

Fill up your 3 copies of BIR form 1906 for the application for Authority to Print Invoices/Receipts. Submit to the attending officer.

nnnn

STEP #7: Additional Step for Mixed Income Earners

nnnn

If it is the case that you are a mixed income earner, you will have to secure 2 columnar books and have them stamped by the guard at the RDO entrance. Do this before proceeding to write all necessary information.

nnnn

STEP 8: Getting Your Books of Account

nnnn

Afterwards, secure and fill up 4 book of accounts (journal, ledger, cash receipts, and cash disbursement), which you can purchase for P200 at the RDO.

nnnn

STEP 9: Finding an Authorized Company to Print Receipts

nnnn

Next, you will be asked to proceed to an authorized printing press outside BIR to print out the receipt booklets. The printing press typically charges 1,000-P1,500 for the receipt booklets. After securing your booklets of receipt and ledger, go back to the RDO and have it stamped by an attendant.

nnnn

NOTE:  As for the processing of receipts by the printing press, it normally takes 1-2 weeks. It may take you longer or shorter depending on the volume of the applicants. For the meantime, ask the printing press to give you a sample receipt under your name.

nnnn

STEP #10: Latest Additional Step: The Taxpayer’s Initial Briefing

nnnn

Meanwhile, your designated RDO will schedule a taxpayer’s briefing for you to know your rights and obligations. The seminar often takes place once a week. Make sure that you show up. Your attendance is required before they release your Certificate of Registration (COR) or BIR Form 2303.

nnnn

NOTE: Do not fall prey. Take whatever freebie they give you (e.g. calendar). Apparently, later on, it would serve as their proof that you truly attended the seminar.

nnnn

STEP #11: Obtaining your Certificate of Registration (COR) or BIR Form 2303

nnnn

Go back to the printing press to get your printed receipts, and proceed to the RDO to submit them together with your books of accounts. Upon checking your proof of attendance, you can obtain your BIR Form 2303, or your Certificate of Registration (COR). Fundamentally, this COR will reflect all the returns that you must file and the taxes you are obliged to pay. Your “Ask for a Receipt” Notice (ARN) will be released together with your COR.

nnnn

STEP #12:  Stamping of the COR

nnnn

The attending officer will ask you to pay for the mandatory registration requirements, which include the P15 Certification fee and the P15 Documentary Stamp. Ask the guard where they sell the Documentary Stamp and proceed there to have your COR stamped. After accomplishing this, the officer will give you a form, which you will attach to the registration certificate later on.

nnnn

The stamping of the COR marks the end of the daunting BIR registration process, which requires a great deal of patience and time considering that the entire registration journey may take you about 5-8 working days. So, you must consider securing all needed documents and strategically scheduling your visit.

nnnn

nnnn

Source: https://www.taxumo.com

nnnn

nnnn

nnnn

Other Reminders

nnnn
  • Know your BIR Revenue District Office (RDO)
nnnn

A mistake often committed by self-employed and mixed income earner physicians is failing to check if their TIN is in the correct BIR Revenue District Office (RDO). The correct RDO, as it happens, is the place where you conducted/will conduct your business/professional service. So it really is not the place where you used to work or where you obtained your TIN ID. If it is not in the right place, you have to apply for a transfer of TIN.

nnnn

To illustrate this better, say for instance you work in Quezon City.  So your TIN number would be in RDO-038. However, you are conducting/will be conducting your business/professional service in Taguig City (RDO-044).  Such an instance calls for a TIN transfer, in this case, from RDO-38 to RDO-044. 

nnnn
  • Obtain Your Professional Tax Receipt
nnnn

One general requirement for doctors applying for a registration with BIR is a Professional Tax Receipt (PTR). Fundamentally, PTRs are available from the Treasury Department of the City Hall where you live. To get a PTR, simply present your PRC license and pay for the corresponding fee, which ranges from P300-P500. Fees may vary depending on the location of the Local Government Unit (LGU) where you are going to obtain your PTR.

nnnn
  • Secure BIR Forms
nnnn

One way to get secure a BIR form is, certainly, to go to a BIR RDO. But to help you save time, you can secure downloadable forms online. The links to which you can access below. Refer to these as you proceed with the 12-step registration process.

nnnn

nnnn

n

posts.title: 8% BIR Tax Rule That Doctors Need to Know
posts.excerpt: The Philippine Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) has released this May 2018 a new Revenue Memorandum Order (RMO) covering the availment of the 8% income tax rate option for professionals and self-employed individuals. This includes our fellow medical practitioners.
posts.html:
n

The Philippine Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) has released this May 2018 a new Revenue Memorandum Order (RMO) covering the availment of the 8% income tax rate option for professionals and self-employed individuals. This includes our fellow medical practitioners.

nnnn

The BIR memo (RMO 23-2018) provides clear guidelines and instructions on how to avail of the 8% income tax rate, under the approved TRAIN tax law of the Philippines.

nnnn

Here are highlights of BIR’s RMO 23-2018.

nnnn

Eight Percent (8%) Income Tax Rate Option for Individuals

nnnn

“1. In general, income of self-employed individuals (includes Single Proprietors, Professionals and Mixed Income Earners) is subject to the graduated income tax rates as provided under Section 24 (A)(2)(a) of the NIRC, as amended.

nnnn

2. Self-employed individuals earning income purely from self-employment/business and/or practice of profession whose gross sales and/or receipts and other non-operating income does not exceed the Value-Added Tax (VAT) threshold of Three Million Pesos (P 3,000,000.00) shall have the option to avail of the following:

nnnn

a. Graduated income tax rates as provided under Section 24 (A)(2)(a) of the NIRC, as amended; or

nnnn

b. Eight percent (8%) tax on gross sales or receipts and other non-operating income in excess of Two Hundred Fifty Thousand Pesos (P 250,000.00) in lieu of the graduated income tax rates and percentage tax under Section 116 under the NIRC, as amended.

nnnn

3. Individuals earning income both from compensation and from self-employment (business or practice of profession) or Mixed Income Earners shall apply the following income tax rates:

nnnn

3.1 The compensation income shall be subject to graduated income tax rates as provided under Section 24 (A)(2)(a) of the NIRC, as amended; and

nnnn

3.2 The income from business or practice of profession shall be subject to the following:

nnnn

a. If the gross sales/receipts and other non-operating income do not exceed the VAT threshold, the individual has the option to be taxed at:

nnnn

i. Graduated income tax rates as provided under Section 24 (A)(2)(a) of the NIRC, as amended; or

nnnn

ii. Eight percent (8%) income tax rate based on gross sales/receipts and other non-operating income in lieu of the graduated income tax rates and percentage tax under Section 116 of the NIRC, as amended.

nnnn

b. If the gross sales/receipts and other non-operating income exceeds the VAT threshold, the individual shall be subject to the graduated income tax rates prescribed under Section 24 (A)(2)(a) of the NIRC, as amended.

nnnn

4. The following criteria should all be satisfied to be able to qualify and avail of the 8% income tax rate option:

nnnn

a. Individuals (Single Proprietor or Professional or Mixed Income Earner) earning from self-employment and/or practice of profession;

nnnn

b. Taxpayers whose gross sales/receipts and other non-operating income did not exceed the P3,000,000 VAT threshold during the taxable year;

nnnn

c. Taxpayers registered and subject only to percentage tax under Section 116 of the NIRC, as amended; or taxpayers exempt from VAT or other percentage taxes; and

nnnn

d. Must have signified their intention to elect the 8% income tax rate thru any of the enumerations under Section II (7) of this Order.

nnnn

5. The 8% income tax rate option is NOT available to the following individual taxpayers and correspondingly shall be taxed based on the graduated income tax rates prescribed under Section 24 (A)(2)(a) of the NIRC, as amended.

nnnn

a. Purely Compensation Income Earners;

nnnn

b. VAT-registered taxpayers, regardless of the amount of gross sales or receipts and other non-operating income;

nnnn

c. Taxpayers exempt from VAT or other percentage taxes whose gross sales/receipts and other non-operating income exceeded the P3,000,000 VAT threshold during the taxable year;

nnnn

d. Taxpayers who are subject to Other Percentage Taxes under Title V of the Tax Code, as amended, except those subject under Section 116 of the same Title;

nnnn

e. Partners of a General Professional Partnership (GPP);

nnnn

f. Individuals enjoying income tax exemption.

nnnn

Individual person who is exempted from income tax such as those registered Barangay Micro Business Enterprises (BMBEs) is bound to the choice that it made to avail itself the privilege under Republic Act No. 9178 for the entire period of its registration with the BIR pursuant to Department Order No. 17-04.

nnnn

The BMBE cannot avail both BMBE status (exempted from income tax, but liable to other internal revenue tax) and the 8% income tax rate option (in lieu of the graduated income tax rates and percentage tax) at the same time, since taxpayers are not allowed to avail of double or multiple tax exemption under different laws, unless specifically provided by law.

nnnn

6. At the beginning of each taxable year, all individuals are subjected to graduated income tax rates as provided under Section 24 (A)(2)(a) of the NIRC, as amended. Self-employed individuals who opted to avail of the 8% income tax rate is effective only for the current taxable year when the election has been made, and shall be automatically subjected to the graduated income tax rates at the beginning of the succeeding taxable years. Thus, the availment of the 8% income tax rate option is required to be signified and selected every taxable year, if the taxpayer wishes to be covered by such income tax rate.

nnnn

7. Self-employed individuals shall signify the intention to elect the 8% income tax rate in filing any of the following:

nnnn

7.1 New Business Registrant

nnnn

a. Upon registration using BIR Form No. 1901 and/or 1701Q; or

nnnn

b. On the initial quarter return (BIR Form No. 2551Q and/or 1701Q) of the taxable year after the commencement of a new business/practice of profession.

nnnn

7.2 Existing Individual Business Taxpayers

nnnn

a. Filing of BIR Form 1905 (Application for Registration Information Update) at the
beginning of the taxable year, to end-date the form type of quarterly percentage tax. Provided that an option to avail the eight percent income tax rate shall be selected in filing the initial quarterly income tax return for income tax purposes; or

nnnn

b. 1st Quarterly Percentage Tax Return; and/or

nnnn

c. 1st Quarterly Income Tax Return.

nnnn

Otherwise, the graduated income tax rates shall apply.

nnnn

8. The income tax rate option, once elected, shall be irrevocable, and no amendment of option shall be made for the taxable year it has been made.

nnnn

Sample: Mr. A elected the 8% income tax rate option on February 14, 2019 by filing BIR Form 1905. Later, before the due date on filing of the 1st Quarterly Percentage Tax Return or 1st Quarterly Income Tax Return, Mr. A had a change of heart to be taxed at the graduated rates. Can Mr. A signify the option to be taxed at the graduated rates in filing his 1st Quarterly Percentage Tax Return or 1st Quarterly Income Tax Return or re-filing the BIR Form 1905 before filing his 1st Quarterly Percentage Tax Return?

nnnn

The answer is no. The election made on February 14, 2019 is irrevocable and no amendment of option shall be made for the said taxable year. Beginning 2020, Mr. A is automatically subjected to the graduated income tax rates, unless opted to elect the 8% income tax rate by signifying his intention.

nnnn

9. Self-employed individual who is qualified and availed of the 8% income tax rate option is:

nnnn

a. Required to file the Quarterly Income Tax Return, unless exempted by any revenue issuances;

nnnn

b. Required to file the Annual Income Tax Return [Financial Statement (FS) is not required to be attached];

nnnn

c. Not required to file the Quarterly Percentage Tax Return;

nnnn

d. Required to signify the intention to avail the 8% income tax rate every taxable year.

nnnn

e. Required to maintain books of accounts and issue receipts/invoices;

nnnn

A taxpayer shall automatically be subjected to graduated income tax rates, liable to VAT prospectively and attached an audited FS in filing of the annual income tax return, if the gross annual sales and/or receipts exceed the amount of Three Million Pesos (P 3,000,000.00) anytime during the current taxable year when the option was made. S/he shall immediately update his/her registration within the month following the month s/he exceeded the VAT threshold to reflect the change in tax profile from non-VAT to a VAT taxpayer. Percentage tax shall be imposed from the beginning of the year until taxpayer is liable to VAT.

nnnn

10. Taxable income for individuals earning income from self-employment/practice of profession shall be based on:

nnnn

a. The net taxable income, if taxpayer opted to be taxed at graduated rates or has failed to signify the 8% income tax rate option;

nnnn

b. The gross sales/receipts and other non-operating income in excess of P250,000, if the 8% income tax rate is availed by self-employed individuals earning income from purely self-employment and/or practice of profession. However, in case of Mixed Income Earner, the taxable income is based on the gross sales/receipts and other non-operating income without the P250,000 reductions, if the option availed is the 8% income tax rate.

nnnn

P250,000 Reduction from Taxable Gross Sales/Receipts and Other Non-Operating Income

nnnn

11. The provision under Section 24(A)(2)(b) of the Tax Code, as amended, which allows an option of 8% income tax rate on gross sales/receipts and other non-operating income in excess of P250,000.00 is available only to self-employed individuals earning income purely from self-employment and/or practice of profession. The P250,000 reductions from taxable gross sales/receipts and other non-operating income is not applicable to mixed income earners since it is already incorporated in the first tier of the graduated income tax rates applicable to compensation income.

nnnn

12. For Mixed Income Earner, the excess of the P250,000.00 over the actual taxable compensation income is not deductible against the taxable income from business/practice of profession under the 8% income tax rate option.

nnnn

The total tax due shall be the sum of the tax due from compensation computed using the graduated income tax rates and the tax due from self-employment/practice of profession resulting from the multiplication of the 8% income tax rate with the total of the gross sales/receipts and other non-operating income.”

nnnn

Source:

nnnn

Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR www.bir.gov.ph)

nnnnn

posts.title: Hyperacidity: The flames in your tummy
posts.excerpt: One of the most common gastric problems faced by people is hyperacidity. And face it, it is very uncomfortable. The stomach normally secretes acids, which are essential for the digestion of food. However, due to various reasons, there are moments when stomach produces excessive acids, which then, causes the flames in your tummy.
posts.html:
n

One of the most common gastric problems faced by people is hyperacidity. And face it, it is very uncomfortable. The stomach normally secretes acids, which are essential for the digestion of food. However, due to various reasons, there are moments when stomach produces excessive acids, which then, causes the flames in your tummy.

nnnn

Hyperacidity has various symptoms, which can include sudden stomach pain, vomiting, loss of appetite, flatulence and heartburn. Sometimes, the problem aggravates and lead to other complications such as chronic indigestion and gastric ulcers.

nnnn

Many can cause hyperacidity, and it can be your eating habits. The way you eat and what you eat are some of the important factors that you must pay close attention to. Smoking and alcohol can also be contributing factor to hyperacidity, as well as stress and medicines that you take.

nnnn

Recommended food to eat

nnnn
  • Fruits and vegetables
  • Lightly seasoned baked, broiled or steamed food
  • Reduced-fat dairy foods like yogurt and cottage cheese
  • Fresh lean cuts of beef, pork, poultry and fish in small portions
  • Low fat whole grains like brown rice, whole oats, whole wheat, and quinoa
nnnn

Do not forget the water – it helps dilutes stomach acids and improves functioning of the entire digestive system. It also helps sooth and cool the burning sensation one feels in the throat and stomach.

nnnn
nnnn

Foods and Drinks to Avoid

nnnn
  • Citrus fruits such as lemons, tangerines, oranges
  • Fried and fatty food
  • Spicy food
  • Tomatoes and tomato sauce
  • Whole fat milk, high fat creams, chocolate milk
  • Strongly flavored cheese
  • Caffeinated beverages, soft drinks and alcohol
nnnnnnnn

Diet plays an important role in controlling stomach acid. So, make sure that you steer clear of what can cause reaction within your stomach. Do not hesitate to reach out to your doctor.

nnnn

Sources:

nnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnn

posts.title: Exercise and What it Does to Your Lungs
posts.excerpt: Love life and engage in regular exercise. This is one of the best things you can do for yourself and your health; has a lot of beneficial effects and promotes improvement of sense of well-being, and longevity. Aside from your heart, muscles and bones, the respiratory system is the most involved part of your body during the exercise. It meets the needs of your working muscles and make sure that there is enough oxygen while it transports carbon dioxide out of your system. According to fitnessVigil, here are the respiratory benefits that you are guaranteed to achieve as you involve yourself in regular exercise.
posts.html:
n

Love life and engage in regular exercise. This is one of the best things you can do for yourself and your health; has a lot of beneficial effects and promotes improvement of sense of well-being, and longevity. Aside from your heart, muscles and bones, the respiratory system is the most involved part of your body during the exercise. It meets the needs of your working muscles and make sure that there is enough oxygen while it transports carbon dioxide out of your system. According to fitnessVigil, here are the respiratory benefits that you are guaranteed to achieve as you involve yourself in regular exercise.

nnnn
    nnnn

    Short-term Benefits of Exercise on the Respiratory System

    nnnn
    • Increases the Respiratory Rate
    nnnn

    Exercise is nothing but an elevated physical activity, due to which the muscles in the body utilize more oxygen and produce excess carbon dioxide. The lungs need to work harder to cater to this oxygen demand, as well as to eliminate the excess carbon dioxide. Hence, the breathing rate of a person increases from about 15 breaths per minute when at rest, to about 40 - 60 breaths per minute during a workout.

    nnnn
    • Increases the Tidal Volume
    nnnn

    The tidal volume refers to the amount of air inhaled and exhaled in one breath. With exercise generating excess carbon dioxide, and with the requirement of more oxygen, the breathing rate elevates immediately after exercising. This elevated breathing rate increases the tidal volume as well.

    nnnn
    • Increases the Rate of Gas Exchange
    nnnn

    There is a continuous exchange of oxygen and carbon dioxide that takes place in the lungs. While exercising, the breathing rate increases, and the rate of gas exchange between the alveoli and capillaries is also maximized to supply oxygen and remove excess carbon dioxide.

    nnnn

    Long-term Benefits of Exercise on the Respiratory System

    nnnn
    • Strengthening of the Respiratory Muscles
    nnnn

    The respiratory muscles, diaphragm, and the intercostal muscles need to work harder for the inhalation and exhalation of gases to occur rapidly. Thus, they are strengthened and the chest cavity becomes larger.

    nnnn
    • Increases Respiratory Volume
    nnnn

    Respiratory volume is the amount of air inhaled, exhaled, and stored in the lungs at any given time. It is also known as Lung Capacity. Physical activity augments the respiratory volume, and increases the volume of oxygen diffused in the cells. This increases the maximum amount of oxygen the body can use during a specified period, which is known as the aerobic capacity of the lungs.

    nnnn
    • Efficient Gaseous Exchange
    nnnn

    Regular exercise increases the number of capillaries surrounding the alveoli. It also makes the capillaries dilate more, so that the gaseous exchange between the two takes place more effectively.

    nnnn
    • Improves Lung Efficiency
    nnnn

    Long-term benefits of exercising also help improve the efficiency of the lungs by facilitating transport of oxygen to all the cells of the body. Training also increases blood flow to a large extent, which enhances the area of the lungs involved in the exchange of gases.

    nnnn


    Elevated levels of oxygen in the body improves your ability to focus, concentrate, and keeps you alert. Exercise enhances your immune power, thus reducing the chances of contacting infections. It also is beneficial for the overall working and metabolism in the body.

    nnnn

    Other than these, an exercise routine is seen beneficial to smoking cessation.
